preview_text:
The model rates this as a tight three-way contest, with Portugal marginally favored at 35% to win, Colombia at 34%, and a draw at 32%. The two teams are separated by only 1 percentage point in win probability, suggesting competitive balance across the full range of plausible outcomes.

The draw probability of 32% reflects genuine uncertainty about which team will break the deadlock. The model assigns meaningful likelihood to all three results, indicating neither team holds a substantial edge. Portugal's slight numerical lead over Colombia is minimal; this match appears genuinely competitive rather than tilted decisively toward either side.

The market diverges meaningfully from the model. Sportsbook consensus places Portugal at 44% to win—a 9 percentage point gap above the model's 35%—while compressing both Colombia's win probability (to 29%) and draw likelihood (to 28%). Polymarket prices widen the spread further, giving Portugal 47% and Colombia 27%. The dashboard does not adjudicate between these sources; the divergence flags a material difference in how the model and market weight Portugal's chances on neutral ground.
